signals.pain: Store owners answer the same handful of customer questions on repeat all day: where's my order, when will it ship, what's your return policy, can I change my address.
signals.evidence: Thread titled 'What's the one customer question you answer over and over (and wish you didn't)?'; per search-result summary, merchants describe support taking up 'almost half a day just responding to repeats' and 'typing the same few answers all day long'.
signals.selena_solution: AI support agent trained on store policies + order data (Shopify API) that auto-answers WISMO, returns, and shipping questions across email/chat, escalating only edge cases.

signals.pain: Solo store owners drown in customer emails (order tracking, product questions, returns, complaints); response times slip after ad campaigns and peak seasons, causing bad reviews.
signals.evidence: Article claims stores at ~100 orders/month get roughly 30-60 support contacts/month and ~500 orders/month get 150-300 contacts; positions the fix as saving 20+ hours/week. Cited figures are the vendor's own estimates.
signals.selena_solution: Done-for-you AI support inbox: auto-triage + drafted replies + full auto-resolution of tier-1 tickets, sized for stores past ~50 orders/month who can't yet justify a support hire.

signals.pain: 'Where is my order?' inquiries dominate ecommerce support volume and each interaction has real labor cost.
signals.evidence: WISMO benchmark pages surfaced in search report WISMO at roughly 10-40% of support contacts in normal months, climbing to 50%+ during peak periods, with each support interaction estimated at $5-$25 in cost (ranges vary by source across the WISMO benchmark articles found).
signals.selena_solution: Automated order-status flows: proactive tracking notifications plus an AI agent wired to the order system, deflecting the single largest ticket category before it hits the inbox.

signals.pain: Returns and exchanges on Shopify are largely manual: merchants track requests, issue refunds, create new orders for exchanges, and manage shipping logistics themselves.
signals.evidence: Search results on Shopify returns handling describe approvals, label creation, inventory updates, and refunds being handled manually, with return/exchange requests going through support and producing long email threads.
signals.selena_solution: Self-service returns/exchange workflow (portal + automation) integrated with Shopify, cutting the email back-and-forth and manual order creation.

signals.pain: Monthly client reporting is a massive manual time sink: pulling data from ad platforms into spreadsheets/slides instead of doing strategy.
signals.evidence: Article states client reporting averages 8.2 hours per client per month, with 15+ hours for complex clients ('25 clients = one full-time employee doing nothing but reports'); cites 63% of marketers naming data integration their biggest reporting headache and 71% of clients saying reports lack actionable recommendations.
signals.selena_solution: AI reporting agent that pulls platform data, writes the analysis and first-draft narrative per client, reducing human work to a short review - a flagship $5k-$10k build for agencies with 10+ clients.

signals.pain: Even dedicated reporting SaaS leaves agencies doing manual, repetitive work and costs scale painfully per client.
signals.evidence: Reviewers report it is 'difficult to create reports with more robust analytics... without having to manually input data sources', limited custom-dimension configuration, and that monthly costs feel expensive; per-client pricing means ~30 clients can run $500-$700+/month per review-roundup sources.
signals.selena_solution: Custom reporting automation owned by the agency (no per-client SaaS tax): scheduled data pulls + templated AI-written reports across all clients from one master template.

signals.pain: Small agencies and SMBs find full-suite CRM/marketing platforms punishingly expensive and gated: pricing rises with contacts ('a tax on success') and essential features sit behind much higher tiers.
signals.evidence: G2 reviewers cite costs rising quickly as contacts grow and features locked in higher tiers; review-analysis sources report the Starter-to-Professional jump (~$20/seat to ~$890/month) plus a mandatory onboarding fee as the most-cited complaint, pushing first-year cost toward $5,000-$9,000.
signals.selena_solution: Lightweight custom CRM automation stack (pipeline, follow-ups, enrichment) built on tools the client already owns - a one-time $5k-$10k build competing directly with a first-year HubSpot Professional bill.

signals.pain: Ecommerce helpdesk pricing is unpredictable and stacks up: ticket-limit overages, per-resolution AI fees, and feature-gated tiers make monthly bills hard to forecast, especially in peak season.
signals.evidence: G2 reviewer quote: 'It's priced too high compared to the rest of the market and it's support is not helpful.' Pricing-analysis sources report overages of roughly $0.36-$0.40/ticket and AI agent fees around $0.90-$1.00 per resolved conversation, with a $360 Pro plan 'commonly landing closer to $720' with AI usage.
signals.selena_solution: Flat-cost custom AI support automation (owned by the brand, no per-resolution fees) as an alternative or supplement to metered helpdesk AI.

signals.pain: Writing client proposals manually eats senior staff time and slows down sales cycles.
signals.evidence: Article claims most consultants spend 6-8 hours writing each proposal manually; related proposal-statistics sources found in the same search report a median of 4.7 business days from first meeting to proposal delivery at digital agencies, cut ~51% with proposal tooling.
signals.selena_solution: AI proposal generator trained on the agency's past winning proposals, pricing, and case studies - discovery-call notes in, branded draft proposal out in minutes.

signals.pain: Existing proposal software itself adds friction: buggy editors and formatting fights make proposal creation slower, not faster.
signals.evidence: Reviewers describe a frustrating editor with autosave/caching bugs, awkward pricing tables, and one stating the constant reformatting 'added so much time to creating a proposal, that it didn't make sense to use anymore'.
signals.selena_solution: Proposal automation that outputs to formats teams already trust (Docs/PDF/Notion) generated from structured inputs, sidestepping fragile WYSIWYG editors.

signals.pain: Client onboarding is a repetitive admin drain: chasing access, credentials, briefs, and kickoff logistics for every new client.
signals.evidence: Author claims that across dozens of agencies, teams lose 10-15 hours every week onboarding new clients - 'more than a full workday gone to admin' - and that clients can tell when onboarding is messy, costing trust before delivery starts.
signals.selena_solution: Automated onboarding system: intake forms, access-request sequences, kickoff scheduling, asset collection, and CRM/project-tool setup triggered automatically on deal-won.

signals.pain: Slow or disorganized onboarding directly drives client churn in the first months of an engagement.
signals.evidence: Benchmark articles surfaced in this search report that 70% of clients who churn within the first 6 months made the decision during the first two weeks of onboarding, and that businesses with a defined onboarding timeline retain 34% more clients in year one (figures as reported by these benchmark posts).
signals.selena_solution: Onboarding automation framed as retention insurance: a defined, automated first-two-weeks experience (welcome sequence, milestones, status visibility) that protects recurring revenue.

signals.pain: DIY client-workflow tools demand weeks of setup and a steep learning curve, so the onboarding automation businesses want never actually gets built.
signals.evidence: Reviewers describe a 'severe learning curve', workflows that are 'confusing and honestly, a bit scary', and one saying it 'cost way too much time and produced way too much overwhelm for simple tasks'; an ecosystem of paid Dubsado-setup specialists exists because setup is so hard.
signals.selena_solution: Done-for-you implementation is the product: Selena builds and tests the client onboarding/CRM workflows the owner would otherwise spend weeks configuring - direct evidence people pay third parties for setup alone.

signals.pain: Course creators and their community members are stuck in a 'relentless cycle' of answering the same FAQs from new students, making community management tedious.
signals.evidence: Article describes how the relentless cycle of frequently asked questions turns vibrant community interaction into a tedious affair, with seasoned members repeating the same responses; positions an AI chatbot trained on frequent questions as the fix.
signals.selena_solution: AI student-support assistant trained on course content and past Q&A, embedded in the community/email, answering tier-1 student questions instantly.

signals.pain: Course creators juggle content creation, marketing, sales, and customer management alone and end up buried in admin (email tweaks, tech troubleshooting) instead of creating.
signals.evidence: Search results across course-creator overwhelm articles describe creators buried in admin work, tweaking marketing emails and troubleshooting tech issues rather than focusing on their best content; overwhelm/burnout is the recurring theme of the article set.
signals.selena_solution: Creator back-office automation bundle: email/marketing workflows, student onboarding sequences, and support automation so the creator's time goes back to content.

signals.pain: Manual CRM data entry consumes a huge share of selling time and is so hated that reps skip or fabricate entries.
signals.evidence: Statistics roundup claims the average salesperson spends ~13 hours/week on manual data entry (28% of the week); other sources in the same search report 5.5-12 hours/week ranges, 23% naming manual entry the main barrier to CRM use, and 37% of sales staff admitting to fabricating CRM data.
signals.selena_solution: CRM auto-capture automation: call/email/meeting data flows into the CRM automatically (transcription + enrichment), eliminating most manual logging.

signals.source_type: vendor blog on agency content operations
signals.pain: Client content demand consistently outpaces production capacity: single-writer chokepoints, hours-long briefs, and approval bottlenecks while client budgets shrink.
signals.evidence: Articles define the content bottleneck as demand consistently outpacing supply, citing over-reliance on one writer, briefs that consume hours of team time, and note 'every social media agency is hitting the same wall: client demand keeps going up, budgets keep going down'.
signals.selena_solution: AI content production pipeline in the agency's voice: brief-to-draft automation, modular content assembly, and approval routing to multiply output without new hires.

signals.market: SMB support teams (validation for ecommerce/creator support offers)
signals.pain: Even a small software company's support inbox (~104 tickets/week) is dominated by questions repetitive enough for AI to fully resolve - human time is spent on answers that don't need a human.
signals.evidence: Jitbit reports letting AI answer support tickets for a week and it 'one-shotted 70 out of 104' tickets without human intervention; adjacent sources in the same search cite 50-89% tier-1 auto-resolution rates for ecommerce AI support tools.
signals.selena_solution: Proof-point for Selena's support-automation offer: majority-share tier-1 auto-resolution is achievable today, making a $5k-$10k implementation defensible against a part-time support hire.
